perturbation.original_text: 2.4 Depending on the business and market development, Party A has the right to formulate or amend the business standards, management measures, quality and/or service standards, and communicate the same to Party B in an appropriate form for Party B to comply with. At the same time, these documents will be used by Party A as basis to manage and assess Party B's business operations. The business standards, management measures, quality and/or service standards so formulated or amended shall be equally effective as this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 Depending on the business and market development, Party A has the right to formulate or amend the business standards, operational directives, quality and/or service standards, and communicate the same to Party B in an appropriate form for Party B to comply with. At the same time, these documents will be used by Party A as basis to manage and assess Party B's business operations. The business standards, operational directives, quality and/or service standards so formulated or amended shall be considered as guidelines but not binding as this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term **'management measures'** has been replaced with **'operational directives,'** and the phrase **'equally effective as this Agreement'** has been changed to **'considered as guidelines but not binding as this Agreement.'** This creates a contradiction because initially, the standards were binding, but now they are merely guidelines. This creates uncertainty regarding the enforceability of these standards against Party B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Depending on the business and market development, Party A has the right to formulate or amend the business standards, operational directives, quality and/or service standards, and communicate the same to Party B in an appropriate form for Party B to comply with. At the same time, these documents will be used by Party A as basis to manage and assess Party B's business operations. The business standards, operational directives, quality and/or service standards so formulated or amended shall be considered as guidelines but not binding as this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the enforceability of the standards formulated by Party A. The original text stated that these standards would be 'equally effective as this Agreement,' implying they are binding. The changed text states they should be 'considered as guidelines but not binding as this Agreement,' directly contradicting the original binding nature. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting the direct in-text contradiction that arises from the modification, creating uncertainty about the enforceability of these standards against Party B, fulfilling the condition for 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: 3.5 Party B shall comply with the various rules and requirements in the business standards, management measures, quality and/or service standards formulated or amended by Party A, which shall be equally binding as this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.5 Party B shall consider the various rules and requirements in the business standards, management policies, quality and/or service standards formulated or amended by Party A. However they will not be considered as binding as this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term **'management measures'** has been replaced with **'management policies,'** and the phrase **'equally binding as this Agreement'** has been changed to **'However they will not be considered as binding as this Agreement.'** This introduces a contradiction. Previously, the standards were binding on Party B, but now they are not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.5 Party B shall consider the various rules and requirements in the business standards, management policies, quality and/or service standards formulated or amended by Party A. However they will not be considered as binding as this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text is identical to the contradicted text. While the explanation describes a change that would create a contradiction (standards being binding vs. not binding), the provided 'Contradicted Text' snippet reflects the *changed* text, not the original. Therefore, based solely on the provided snippets, there is no in-text contradiction, since both sections state the same thing: standards are not binding.

perturbation.original_text: 13.3 Any business rules, management measures, quality standards and/or customer service standards formulated by Party A during the term hereof shall be part of this Agreement, and if such rules, measures and/or standards conflict with the terms hereof, such rules, measures and/or standards shall prevail, unless the Parties deem it appropriate to apply this Agreement or deem it necessary to execute a separate agreement for such conflicts.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.3 Any business rules, guiding principles, quality standards and/or customer service standards formulated by Party A during the term hereof shall be considered supplementary to this Agreement, and if such rules, measures and/or standards conflict with the terms hereof, the guiding principles shall not prevail, and the terms of this agreement shall prevail.
perturbation.explanation: The term **'management measures'** has been replaced with **'guiding principles,'** and the phrase **'such rules, measures and/or standards shall prevail'** has been changed to **'the guiding principles shall not prevail, and the terms of this agreement shall prevail.'** This introduces a contradiction as the original agreement states that the standards will prevail if they conflict with the original agreement, whereas the new text now states that the terms of the agreement prevail. This contradiction creates uncertainty over which takes precedence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.3 Any business rules, guiding principles, quality standards and/or customer service standards formulated by Party A during the term hereof shall be considered supplementary to this Agreement, and if such rules, measures and/or standards conflict with the terms hereof, the guiding principles shall not prevail, and the terms of this agreement shall prevail.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 13.3 to state that if business rules, guiding principles, quality standards, and customer service standards formulated by Party A conflict with the terms of the agreement, the terms of the agreement prevail. The original text stated that such rules, measures, and standards would prevail in case of conflict. This creates a direct contradiction within the same section because one version states the standards prevail, while the other states the agreement prevails, and this section is also the 'Contradicted Text'.
